title: Increased sensitivity of Euplotes crassus to selective agents using glucose culture conditions
author: Lawrence A. Klobutcher
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.2ahgab6

steps.instruction: Distribute a dense culture of Dunaliella salina to two 50 ml screw-cap centrifuge tubes, and pellet the algae by centrifugation at 1,000 RPM (~200 x g) for 2 min. in a clinical centrifuge.
Remove all but the last 2.5 ml of supernatant from each tube, resuspend the algae pellets, and combine.

To the above tube, add 2.5 ml of a log phase culture of Euplotes crassus (grown with Dunaliella in artificial seawater as the food source ), and 45 ml of 0.3 M glucose (freshly prepared using deionized water).
Add selective agent to desired final concentration. 200 ug/ul G418, 200 ug/ml paromomycin, or 40 ug/ml puromycin.
Cells can be distributed microtiter plates. For example, 2 ml per well for a 24-well microtiter plate.
Incubate at room temperature in a humidified chamber (e.g., plastic box with wet paper towels or beaker of water). Cell death occurs within 2-3 days.
